evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Batch to my iOS app. Here's my empty view controller: EmptyViewController.swift. I need to scan EAN-13 and QR barcodes. Please show me the complete updated view controller code in your response.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, returns complete BarcodeBatch integration code inline (DataCaptureContext via DataCaptureContext.initialize + .shared, Camera.default with BarcodeBatch.recommendedCameraSettings, BarcodeBatch(context:settings:), DataCaptureView added as subview, BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay, BarcodeBatchListener with barcodeBatch(_:didUpdate:frameData:), lifecycle on viewWillAppear/viewWillDisappear, removeListener in deinit), and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding ScanditBarcodeCapture and ScanditCaptureCore via Swift Package Manager
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Info.plist
A license key placeholder string is present
import ScanditBarcodeCapture is added
DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey: is called and DataCaptureContext.shared is used
BarcodeBatchSettings is constructed and configured
Symbology .ean13UPCA is enabled via settings.set(symbology:enabled:)
Symbology .qr is enabled
Camera.default is used to obtain the camera
BarcodeBatch.recommendedCameraSettings is applied via camera?.apply(_:completionHandler:)
context.setFrameSource(camera, completionHandler:) is called
BarcodeBatch(context: settings:) convenience initializer is used (not a factory like .forDataCaptureContext)
barcodeBatch.addListener( is called
DataCaptureView(context:frame:) is used and added with view.addSubview
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay(barcodeBatch:view:) is called
BarcodeBatchListener is implemented with barcodeBatch(_:didUpdate:frameData:)
The didUpdate callback dispatches UI work via DispatchQueue.main.async
viewWillAppear turns the camera on (camera?.switch(toDesiredState: .on)) and sets barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true
viewWillDisappear turns the camera off (camera?.switch(toDesiredState: .off)) and sets barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false
deinit calls barcodeBatch.removeListener(self)

evals.prompt: I want to highlight EAN-13 barcodes in green and CODE128 barcodes in blue in my MatrixScan Batch view controller. Here is my view controller: IntegratedViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill conforms to BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayDelegate, implements barcodeBatchBasicOverlay(_:brushFor:) to return colored Brushes per symbology, and assigns overlay.delegate = self.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayDelegate is adopted (e.g. via an extension)
barcodeBatchBasicOverlay(_:brushFor:) is implemented
.ean13UPCA returns a green Brush
.code128 returns a blue Brush
overlay.delegate = self (or equivalent) is assigned
Response mentions that per-barcode brush customization requires the MatrixScan AR add-on license
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch initializer, DataCaptureView, BarcodeBatchListener, camera lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch view controller I want to log all currently tracked barcodes — their tracking identifier and data — on every frame. Here is my view controller: IntegratedViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill updates barcodeBatch(_:didUpdate:frameData:) to iterate over session.trackedBarcodes and log each TrackedBarcode's identifier and barcode.data, copying data out of the session before DispatchQueue.main.async.

assertions.text: session.trackedBarcodes is accessed
Each TrackedBarcode's identifier is used
Each TrackedBarcode's barcode.data is used
Data is copied out of the session before DispatchQueue.main.async (session.trackedBarcodes is not accessed inside the async block)
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch initializer, camera setup, overlay, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Batch to my SwiftUI app. Here's my empty ContentView: ContentView.swift. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code128 barcodes. Please show me the complete updated code in your response.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes the SwiftUI path, creates a UIKit ScanViewController containing all BarcodeBatch APIs (context, mode, settings, camera, DataCaptureView, BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay, BarcodeBatchListener, lifecycle, deinit cleanup), and wraps it in a UIViewControllerRepresentable. The SwiftUI View struct contains no Scandit-specific code beyond using the representable. The setup checklist is shown.

assertions.text: A UIViewControllerRepresentable is defined that wraps a UIKit UIViewController

All BarcodeBatch APIs (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeBatch, BarcodeBatchSettings, DataCaptureView, BarcodeBatchListener, camera lifecycle) live inside the UIKit view controller, NOT inside the SwiftUI View struct
The SwiftUI View struct contains no Scandit-specific code beyond referencing the representable
import ScanditBarcodeCapture is added
DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey: is called and DataCaptureContext.shared is used
Symbology .ean13UPCA is enabled via settings.set(symbology:enabled:)
Symbology .code128 is enabled
Camera.default is used and BarcodeBatch.recommendedCameraSettings is applied via camera?.apply(_:completionHandler:)
context.setFrameSource(camera, completionHandler:) is called
BarcodeBatch(context: settings:) convenience initializer is used
DataCaptureView is created and added with view.addSubview inside the view controller
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay(barcodeBatch:view:) is called
BarcodeBatchListener is implemented with barcodeBatch(_:didUpdate:frameData:)
The didUpdate callback dispatches UI work via DispatchQueue.main.async
viewWillAppear turns the camera on and sets barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true
viewWillDisappear turns the camera off and sets barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false
deinit calls barcodeBatch.removeListener(self)
Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding ScanditBarcodeCapture and ScanditCaptureCore via Swift Package Manager
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Info.plist
A license key placeholder string is present

evals.prompt: I want to show a small label above each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan Batch view controller — the label should display the barcode's data. Here is my view controller: IntegratedViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay to the view controller, conforms to BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayDelegate, implements barcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay(_:viewFor:) to return a UILabel (or other UIView) showing trackedBarcode.barcode.data, and mentions that BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay requires the MatrixScan AR add-on license. Existing integration code (basic overlay, listener, lifecycle) is preserved.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay(barcodeBatch:view:) is created and stored on the view controller
advancedOverlay.delegate = self (or equivalent) is assigned
BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayDelegate is adopted (e.g. via an extension)
barcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay(_:viewFor:) is implemented and returns a UIView (e.g. UILabel) that displays trackedBarcode.barcode.data
Response mentions that BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay requires the MatrixScan AR add-on license
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch initializer, BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay, BarcodeBatchListener, camera lifecycle) is preserved
